channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas releases 3 frail-looking Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners under Gaza ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas-led militants released three gaunt, frail-looking Israeli hostages and Israel freed nearly 200 Palestinian prisoners Saturday in the latest exchange of a ceasefire that has paused 16 months of war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says some white South Africans are oppressed, could be resettled in the US. They say no thanks
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — Groups representing some of South Africa's white minority responded Saturday to a plan by President Donald Trump to offer them refugee status and resettlement in the United States by saying: thanks, but no thanks.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Where the fight over USAID's future stands
item.description: The future of the government's foreign assistance agency hangs in the balance after a federal judge temporarily paused the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of employees on leave Friday night. Unions representing federal employees took the fight to save the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Leaders from eastern and southern Africa call for a ceasefire and negotiations in Congo
item.description: Leaders from eastern and southern Africa have called for an immediate and unconditional ceasefire in eastern Congo, where Rwanda-backed rebels are threatening to overthrow the Congolese government.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Muhumuza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Russian spy ship caught fire off Syria's coast, officials say. Here's audio of its broadcasts
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The man is insistent: Our ship is in difficulty, so keep your distance, he instructs another vessel over the radio. “Warship on your course,” he says. "I am drifting. I’m not under command.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he wants to negotiate about Ukraine. It's not clear if Putin really does
item.description: Nearly three years after President Vladimir Putin invaded Ukraine, his troops are making steady progress on the battlefield.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A family fleeing violence in eastern Congo sees little hope as rebels grab more land
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — None of Zawadi Sifa's seven children have known life without fear and the constant threat of violence.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department lays out plans for $7 billion-plus arms sale to Israel as Netanyahu visits DC
item.description: WASHINGTON — The State Department has formally told Congress that it plans to sell more than $7 billion in weapons to Israel, including thousands of bombs and missiles, just two days after President Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House.
item.dc:creator: By LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: President Trump says 'we will have relations with North Korea'; it's a 'big asset' that he gets along with Kim
item.description: President Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba at the White House and touted his good relationship with Kim Jong Un from meetings in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil's socialist president tells citizens not to buy expensive grocery items as food prices rise
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said in a video message posted to social media that citizens should not buy expensive food items to deal with rising food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: We will have relations with North Korea
item.description: President Trump on Friday said the U.S. will have relations with North Korea, touting his personal relationship with Kim Jong Un, who rules the nuclear-armed country with an iron fist and has soldiers fighting on the front lines of Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A look at the hostages taken to Gaza by Hamas, by the numbers
item.description: Israel and Hamas are in the midst of a six-week ceasefire during which Hamas is releasing dozens of the hostages captured in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump sanctions on ICC protects US military and government officials, former NSC staffer says
item.description: Amid the International Criminal Court’s issuance of arrest warrants for Israeli leaders for their role in waging a self-defense war against Hamas, President Donald Trump punished the global body, prompting strong reactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: São Paulo, Brazil small plane crash kills 2
item.description: A small plane crashed into a bus on a road in São Paulo, Brazil, on Friday, killing the pilot and co-pilot and injuring two people on the ground, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Autocratic regimes 'relishing' USAID gutting: Samantha Power
item.description: Samantha Power, a former administrator of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), says autocratic regimes across the world are “relishing” the Trump administration's efforts to gut the agency. “It's not even an opinion.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: International Criminal Court condemns Trump-imposed sanctions
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) on Friday condemned President Trump’s executive order imposing sanctions on the global tribunal. Trump ordered the sanctions in response to the ICC issuing arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Israel's former defense chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: DOJ disbands foreign influence task force, unit tasked with seizing Russian oligarchs' asset
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi disbanded a Biden-era initiative targeting Russian oligarchs as well as another designed to combat foreign influence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Frozen US aid brings new challenges to groups hit by harsh crackdowns in Russia and Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Rights groups, activists and independent media in Russia and Belarus endured increased government scrutiny, repressive laws and even being outlawed and forced to operate from exile abroad. Many survived despite the difficulties.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels tell residents of a captured eastern Congolese city they are safe
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The Rwanda-backed rebels who captured eastern Congo 's key city of Goma sought to reassure its residents Thursday, holding a stadium rally and promising safety under their administration as they try to shore up public support amid growing international pressure.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, RUTH ALONGA and JEAN-YVES KAMALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, which U.S. Vice President J.D.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AI, scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
item.description: Hundreds of papyrus scrolls were found in the 1750s in the remains of a lavish villa at the Roman town of Herculaneum.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East expert says Israel and the US are back on the same page
item.description: Scholars for Peace in the Middle East Executive Director Asaf Romirowsky spoke with Fox News Digital about how Trump's return to DC is already having an impact on the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting. But a final death toll, a conclusive number of wounded and a motive hadn’t yet been determined hours later.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation
